_comment: Static benchmark text used by the model router when picking a model for a task. Override with LOXIA_BENCHMARKS_PATH env var or ~/.onbuzz/benchmarks.json. Plain text — the router includes this verbatim in its routing prompt.
benchmarkText:
Model selection guide (OnBuzz Community defaults, 2026):

## OpenAI
- gpt-4o: best general-purpose model with vision; strong tools; 128k context. Default for general tasks.
- gpt-4o-mini: fast + cheap general model; tools + vision; 128k context. Use for high-volume / latency-sensitive work.
- o3-mini: reasoning model; strong on math/code/logic; no vision; 200k context. Use for hard reasoning tasks.

## Anthropic
- claude-opus-4-7: top-tier general model; tools + vision; 200k context; excels at long-form code and instruction following. Use for complex agentic work.
- claude-sonnet-4-6: balanced quality/cost; tools + vision; 200k context. Default Anthropic pick.
- claude-haiku-4-5: fast + cheap; tools + vision; 200k context. Use for high-volume work.

## Google Gemini
- gemini-2.0-flash: fast general model with massive 1M context; tools + vision. Use when you need long context cheaply.
- gemini-1.5-pro: high-quality with 2M context; tools + vision. Use for very-long-context tasks.

## xAI
- grok-4: strong reasoning + general tasks; tools; 256k context.
- grok-2-vision: vision + tools; smaller context; balanced cost.

## Local (Ollama)
- Any ollama-* model: free, private, runs locally. Capabilities depend on the underlying model. Use when privacy or zero cost matter more than top-tier quality.

## Routing rules
- Prefer models marked recommended_for the task type (general / tools / vision / reasoning / fast / long-context).
- Tier order for cost-sensitive routing: budget < balanced < premium.
- For vision tasks, only models with supportsVision:true.
- For tool-calling, only models with supportsTools:true (all listed cloud models support tools).
